item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…when subjects are seen preparing raw poultry such as chicken or beef

Beef is not poultry. Poultry means domestic fowl, such as chickens, turkeys, ducks, and geese. Recommend changing to "raw meat such as chicken or beef."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those egg bois are cute, but I feel like the anomaly is way too simple and there isn't much to it. I expected some twist or punchline at the end, but it never came.

Also, I really dislike that -A Addendum. It seems redundant, especially in-universe. I don't think employees of a secret organization that deals with anomalous would do something like this so many times that there would be a need for this addendum.

item.title: Poultry.
item.content:encoded:
or attempting to turn on the stove when subjects are seen preparing raw poultry such as chicken or beef.

I do think that's an incorrect use of poultry as beef is not poultry. aside from that I found your article simply Egg-Cellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I guess in a sense you're right. But at the same time, there are people who would attempt to do that in the foundation. I really want to keep it in because it's a neat little entry, but at the same time, if it makes the story better, then poof, there it goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoopsies! I'll fix it right away, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4209 was specimen told before starting to only put creamer inside the coffee. SCP-4209 followed directions.

Seems like there's a typo.

Nice picture and lovely done! I was hoping some creepy entries as I read through the test log but there turned to be none.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good story, but I highly recommend removing Addendum SCP-4209-A. The Foundation is supposed to be made up of highly skilled and trained research scientists. Unless there is an implication that people taking them home is part of their anomaly, there shouldn't be any threats of "don't do this or risk punishment"; that's 101 level security.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course! It's so cute, still have the egg in my fridge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume you own the picture here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for all the support! For your questions…

1. Yes, they roll around to communicate. They roll up and down for "yes" and roll side to side to communicate "no."

2. No, they can't open drawers, but they can work small appliances if the appliances are laid out for them. For example, they can't open a drawer and pull out a bottle of salt, but they can tip the salt bottle over to season food if the salt bottle is out on a counter.

Also thank you for that picture compliment! My relatives really didn't have any idea what to think when they saw an egg with a smiley face in the morning when making breakfast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the mental picture of a bunch of eggs helping out with cooking, and the story told is quite entertaining. There are a few places with odd jumps1, but nothing that derails the read-through significantly.

I do have one question: can they open drawers or appliances? As in, if you present them with a stocked kitchen, which has all of the implements and ingredients necessary to create the chicken mentioned in the third test (stored away as they would be in a typical kitchen environment), would they be able to pull out everything?

Also, that picture is just adorable!

Footnotes
1. For example, how do the eggs move to answer "yes/no" questions? Do they roll for "yes" and spin for "no"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… a very good point. Don't worry, I'll be sure to revise the article in my free time. You brought up a lot of cool ideas, though! Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How do they move? Can they levitate? Can they levitate other objects? I'm all for these guys just having "invisible limbs" or whatever, but there is no way they can cook most things by just rolling.

The story is adorable, but I feel like this needs to be addressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, I forgot about that, thank you for pointing that out! I kinda left that unanswered like a dumb idiot, sorry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, that's so sweet, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry you didn't enjoy it, thank you for checking out the page though!

item.title: Re: Nice.
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I agree, that part has been left unanswered as of now, but I will try to revise some of it if I can!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have a list of what it's to be fed, but there is no mention of how an egg with a drawn on face eats. Does the smile actually work? Does it absorb the food through the shell? I can't get past that so -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly after reading nearly half of the articles on this site in the last few months, this article just felt like sinking into a freshly made bed. Nothing trying to fuck with my head, no extensive storylines, nothing extremely grimdark, just something simple, cute and wholesome. Thanks

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure about this one. As far as regular anomalies go, this one is pretty uninteresting for me.

item.title: Nice.
item.content:encoded:
This is actually pretty good, I couldn’t find any bad sides nor any other mistakes. The only thing I didn’t understand is are the locals report the SCP to the foundation to capture them or the foundation just found it when they were in a supermarket or something?

Keep it up!

+1
